result.content: FINAL_ANSWER: Perform a post-order traversal of the final tree. For each node u, identify its children v1, v2, ..., vk in the order they appear in the final tree. The merge operations at node u are performed sequentially: first merge v1 and v2 to form a new intermediate node, then merge that intermediate node with v3, and so on, until vk is merged. Output these operations in post-order traversal order to ensure that all subtrees are fully constructed before their parent node attempts to merge them. JUSTIFICATION: A post-order traversal ensures that all child nodes (and their respective internal merge operations) are processed and 'available' before the parent node performs its own merge operations, satisfying the dependency requirements of the tree construction.
